title: Not Every Query Needs the Same Source
concept: Routing a code question to a code index and a policy question to a docs index instead of one shared index for everything.

exercises.prompt: Welcome to Unit 464! Let's start with a quick recap. What is the main goal of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)?

options.text: Retrieving external data to ground an LLM's response
Training a model from scratch on private data
Compressing text files to save storage space

exercises.explain: Before routing queries to multiple sources, simple RAG systems retrieve documents from a single vector store to ground the LLM's responses.

cards.title: Where Should We Look?
cards.body: When you ask a coworker a question, you don't search the entire company archive. You go to the specific person who knows the answer. Let's learn how to make LLMs do the same!

cards.title: What is Query Routing?
cards.body: Query routing is the process where an LLM analyzes an incoming question and decides which specific database or tool is best suited to answer it, before doing any search.

cards.title: The Smart Receptionist
cards.body: Imagine going to a hospital lobby. Instead of sending every visitor to the emergency room, a receptionist directs bone fractures to radiology and billing questions to the front desk. Query routing does the exact same thing for your AI.

cards.title: Routing in Action
cards.body: If a user asks 'How do I reset my password?', the router sends the query to the HR policy index. If they ask 'Write a Python loop', the router switches to the code repository index.

cards.title: The One-Size-Fits-All Trap
cards.body: Many believe putting all data into one giant vector index is best. In reality, mixing code and policy documents in one database dilutes search quality and retrieves irrelevant context.

cards.title: Routed vs. Mixed Indexing
cards.body: Splitting databases keeps search results clean, while combining them creates messy, irrelevant matches.

left.text: Coding query -> Code Index -> Perfect syntax answer

right.text: Coding query -> Mixed Index -> Cluttered code & HR policy text

exercises.prompt: Why is it better to route a query to a specific index rather than searching a combined index?

options.text: It makes the vector database larger and slower.
It prevents irrelevant information from diluting the answer.
It guarantees the LLM will never make a mistake.
It forces the user to write their query in code.

exercises.explain: Directing a question to a specialized database prevents the LLM from getting confused by irrelevant information from other domains.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A query router must query every single database index to find the correct answer.
exercises.explain: Routing allows the system to direct questions to the single most relevant source, avoiding the need to query every index.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing component that decides where the query goes:
exercises.sentence: The system uses a router to send code questions to the code index.
exercises.explain: The router acts as the decision maker, classifying the query and sending it to the correct specialized database.

exercises.prompt: Which of these represents an automated query routing approach in a RAG system?

options.text: Search the master database for 'how to write a function' and return the top 5 results.
Let the LLM classify the query as 'Code' or 'Policy', then query the respective database based on that classification.
Ask the user to manually click button A for code help or button B for policy help before typing.

exercises.explain: Option B is AI-generated because it uses structured JSON to dynamically classify and route a query based on intent, whereas the others are basic static searches.

exercises.prompt: A developer wants to build a hybrid customer support bot that handles both billing questions and python code bugs. What is the most efficient query routing strategy?

options.text: It merges both databases into a single index and searches everything at once
It classifies the user's intent first, then queries only the most relevant database
It translates coding questions into legal policy language
It bypasses the LLM entirely to speed up response times

exercises.explain: Query routing acts as a traffic controller before any search happens. By identifying the query type first, the system avoids polluting the prompt with irrelevant data from the wrong index.
